Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Prove Up Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Anjan Mitra
Off Calendar Nov-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant Anjan Mitra.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:54 a.m.

Mani Sheik, Esq. appeared for plaintiff the Nathan Schatz Family Trust, a California Trust, and with plaintiffs Zachary Nathan and Andrea Schatz, both individually and as Trustees of the Nathan Schatz Family Trust.
Ms. Schatz, along with Messrs. Sheik and Nathan, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Anjan Mitra.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing was filed on April 8, 2020, and personally served on defendant Anjan Mitra on October 12, 2020.
Default as to defendant Anjan Mitra was entered on November 17, 2020.
Notice of Settlement and Stipulation to Retain Jurisdiction as to defendants AEM Food & Beverage, Inc., DOSA Food & Beverage, Inc., DOSA Holdings, Inc., and Emily Mitra was filed on July 22, 2021.
Order Approving the afore-mentioned Settlment was filed on July 22, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, November 18, 2021, via oral motion by Mr. Sheik, who is informed by the Court that the Judgment in this matter will not be processed until the actual Request for Dismissal of the DOE defendants has been filed with the Clerk's Office.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs the Nathan Schatz Family Trust, Zachary Nathan, individually and as Trustee of the Nathan Schatz Family Trust, and Andrea Schatz, individually and as Trustee of the Nathan Schatz Family Trust, and against the defaulted defendant Anjan Mitra, as follows:

Damages - $57,526.15.

Total - $57,526.15.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court, but shall not be processed until the actual Request for Dismissal of the DOE defendants has been filed with the Clerk's Office.

Proceedings concluded at 11:09 a.m.
